observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from paying procurement
contracts which are not yet delivered and accepted.
b) require the supplier to affix the sticker “RESCUE” and the “MDRRMO
TABANGO” and the municipality logo on the vehicle as specified in the
contract agreement;
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) submit explanation to the Audit Team why disbursement voucher was
recommended for approval by the Municipal Mayor despite the
absence of the Inspection and Acceptance Report indicating the
delivery of the item.
b) to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the
overstatement of the Construction and Heavy Equipment and
understatement of the CIB-LCCA account as of December 31, 2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the accountable
officer to liquidate the cash advances as required under COA Circular No.
97-002 and direct the Municipal Accountant to reflect the necessary
adjusting entries to correct the above deficiencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer immediately the unexpended
balance of LDRRMF to a Special Trust Fund account in the Trust Fund books.
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account and transfer
immediately the unspent DRRM fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete inventory of the
Municipality’s property, plant and equipment as soon as possible,
following the guidelines of inventory taking per COA Circular No. 80-124,
to be reported on the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and
for submission to the Audit Team not later than June 30, 2022;
b. direct the Municipal Engineer (acting GSO) and the Municipal
Accountant to maintain a complete and up-dated Property Card and
Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card, respectively, so that all
PPEs are properly accounted for;

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the drugs and medicines and
medical supplies counted by the Audit Team on March 9, 2022. Henceforth,
record subsequent procurement of supplies and materials and drugs and
medicines as an asset under the appropriate Inventory account and recognize
the related expenses based on the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued
prepared by the Supply Officer with copy/ies of pre-numbered Requisition
and Issued Slips duly signed by the end-users.
b. Require the GSO to prepare and submit RSMI duly supported with RIS
monthly to the Accounting Office for recording of issuances of inventory
items; and
c. Require the RHU to prepare a Report of Drugs and Medicines Issued duly
supported by an RIS and to conduct a physical count of undistributed
medicines and other items at least twice a year to be submitted to GSO and
Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Design Engineer and the
Municipal Engineer should ensure inclusion of Project Billboard/Signboard
and Occupational Safety and Health as separate pay items in every
infrastructure project implemented in compliance with COA Circular No.
2013-004 dated January 20, 2013 and DPWH Department Order No. 56 s. 2005
dated April 20, 2005.
We also recommended that Project Billboard/Signboard be set up at the
project site for all the projects of the LGU so that the public is duly notified of
the essential features/elements of the project.
Further, we recommended that compliance on Construction Safety and Health
Program shall be required on all winning bidders to ensure that the workers
of LGU projects are adequately protected against occupational risks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The officers and employees concerned to prepare a completely filled-out
PR, PO and IAR for all LGU procurements;
b. The Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking
documents and a written explanation why the disbursement voucher was
recommended for approval by the Municipal Mayor despite the deficiency;
and henceforth, verify the completeness of the data in the supporting
documents attached to disbursement vouchers such as Purchase Request,
Purchase Orders, and Inspection & Acceptance Reports, etc. before these
are forwarded to the Municipal Treasurer for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant obliged the contractor with
outstanding advances as of this date an irrevocable standby letter of credit, a
bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand equivalent to the
outstanding balances issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed
by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the municipality and to
strictly adhere with the provisions provided in 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officials and
employees concerned to submit the lacking reports and documents to the
Audit Team for review and evaluation within 5 calendar days upon receipt of
this memo, otherwise, a Notice of Suspension shall be issued.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents and a written explanation
why the disbursement vouchers were approved despite the deficiency; and
henceforth, review the completeness of the supporting documents of the
disbursement vouchers before forwarding these to the Municipal Mayor for
approval.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a) review the completeness of the signatures on the disbursement voucher before a
check is prepared and signed, and
b) demand from the suppliers the issuance of official receipts or sales invoice as
acknowledgment of the payment to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and other officials and employees concerned to submit to the
Audit Team the lacking appropriate documents presented in Table XX within
5 days from receipt of this report. This is without prejudice to the issuance of
a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit explanation for
the Audit Team’s evaluation for recommending approval of the disbursement
vouchers by the Municipal Mayor despite the lacking documents. Henceforth,
ensure that all disbursements should be supported with complete
documentations in accordance with the provisions in PD 1445 and COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Chairperson of the
BAC to submit immediately the lacking contract documents within five (5)
days from receipt of this AOM. This is without prejudice to issuance of a
Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.
We also recommended that the BAC exert their best effort that all supporting
documents to the contracts are complete and compliant with the requirements
of Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and
Section 17.1 of the RIRR of RA 9184 and submit to the Audit Team within five
(5) days after perfection of the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountants shall:

Determine and trace back the transactions recorded in the Subsidiary Ledgers which are unknown but shows discrepancies in recording;

Review and monitor the works of the Accounting personnel to maintain reliable balances of the Subsidiary Ledgers; and

Prepare an adjusting entry to reclassify the transactions to Due to LGU account to correct the reported balances of the liabilities accounts.
observations.managementAction: Records were still in the process of verification of balances

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall determine the cash balance under the Trust Fund of the LGU and tally the available cash with the records of the Municipal Accountant.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall:

Prepare the Disbursement Voucher for the return to the National Government of the funds from unimplemented and completed programs and projects; and

Prepare adjusting entries to reclassify to Prior Year’s Adjustment the other balances which are identifiable as income and those arising from erroneous posting.
observations.managementAction:
Cash balances of all Trust Fund account were monitored by the Municipal Treasurer, however, an on-going reconciliation between cash records and books of accounts

The Municipal Accountant coordinated with different National Government Agencies to confirm the amount to be returned

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant with a complete description and cost segregation for each public infrastructure project as basis in reconciling with accounting records and to ensure that all Pubic Infrastructures implemented by the LGU prior to CY 2017 are recorded in the books of accounts.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) reconcile the balances of each project with that of the accounting records from the provided Report of Inventory of Public Infrastructures; (2) identify the specific public infrastructures acquired prior to CY 2017, record the same to the appropriate Infrastructure Asset accounts; (3) maintain property ledger cards for each public infrastructure, and; (4) prepare a lapsing schedule to compute depreciation of each asset.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Engineer is still completing the records to be forwarded to Municipal Accountant

The Municipal Accountant is waiting for the report from the Municipal Engineer to be reconciled with the books of accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Agriculturist immediately  amend the executed Memorandum of Agreement  between the LGU of Tabango and the respective Association to include the provisions defining the accountabilities and responsibilities of both parties as regards to the distributed equipment, livestock, other agricultural supplies and interventions to ensure  that the objectives of the program will be achieved for the benefit of the associations and its members.
observations.managementAction: On the process of amending the MOA between the LGU and its beneficiaries

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, through the SWM Focal Person, undertake the following: 

Establish Materials Recovery Facility in the five barangays in order to facilitate the segregation of solid waste at the barangay level;
observations.managementAction: Only the MRF at Brgy Butason II were still not implemented, but the other MRFs from the remaining four (4) Brgys were already implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards respectively, for each type of inventory item, to monitor receipts and issuance and reconcile the respective balances monthly.
observations.managementAction: On-going maintenance of ledger cards and stock cards

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the above-cited governing rules and regulations in the recording and conduct of physical inventory of property, plant and equipment and shall specifically require the Accounting and Supply Office to maintain the respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards and regularly reconcile their records pertaining to PPE balances to prove, that the assigned values to the assets per the agency’s books are reliable and accurate and to afford fair presentation of the related PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Physical Inventory was conducted, but the records were still in the process of reconciling

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should forward all cancelled checks to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of adjusting journal entries.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should monitor the cancelled checks and the status of outstanding checks and prepare adjusting journal entry to restore in the books the amount of cancelled and outstanding stale checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare an adjusting entry to reclassify the procured rescue ambulance amounting to ₱1,396,254.00 from Motor Vehicle account to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account.

We recommended that the LDRRMO and MSWDO submit the Distribution List duly acknowledged by the beneficiaries which will be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in preparing an adjusting journal entry to record the issuance of all relief goods or inventories.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist should provide the needed documentations on the turned-over of the donated composting facility from the BSWM to the Municipal Accountant.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare a journal entry to record the donated composting facility amounting to ₱849,968.00 under the Agricultural and Forestry Equipment account as soon as the documents are received.

We recommended that the BAC, the Technical Working Group and the Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant should submit all the lacking documents and an explanation for the Audit Team’s evaluation why the project was paid despite the lacking and deficient supporting documents.
For similar transactions in the future, the BAC should see to it that all documents submitted by the bidders are reviewed thoroughly and the eligibility documents are verified and confirmed, the contract is awarded to a qualified bidder with the most complying bid; and a copy of the contract, including supporting documents, be submitted to the Audit Team, for review, within five days from execution.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should deduct retention money from the payments of progress billings of the contractors or require them to post an irrevocable letter of credit, bank guarantees or surety bond callable on demand as substitute for said retention money.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer should supervise regularly the implementation of the infrastructure projects from any uncorrected discovered defects and third party liabilities before the retention money is released.

We recommended that the MSWD Officer, in coordination with the Barangay Officials, maintain an updated database of the constituent families with appropriate profiling, in order to have a readily available data for similar programs of the national government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should ensure that a MOA with the recipient Barangay be executed for financial assistance/fund transfers, which shall contain, among others, the purpose of fund transfers and the duties and responsibilities of both parties.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit the MOA on the funds transferred to Barangay Tabing and Barangay Poblacion amounting to ₱150,000.00 and ₱500,000.00, respectively, together with the authorization from the local Sanggunian for the Local Chief Executive to enter into the said contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall:

Prepare an Adjusting Entry to correct the balances of the Road Network account and its Accumulated Depreciation account in the amount of ₱2,205,975.34 and ₱1,087,008.35, respectively; and

Record depreciation of road network accounts correctly from its initial recognition and subsequent years, including the accurate computation of its estimated useful life without providing for any residual value.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare an adjusting entry in the books of the LGU to correct the reported balances of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall reconcile the records of the Due to GSIS account including the maintenance of an updated Subsidiary Ledger to determine any unremitted collections including the determination of any errors that shall require an adjustment.

We also recommended that the Human Resource Officer shall furnish immediately the GSIS the Notice of Salary Adjustment and Notice of Step Increment to avoid unreconciled records of basic pay between the LGU and the GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman, shall include only in the Annual Investment Plan the projects which can readily be implemented by the LGU during the year, through proper scheduling and prioritization.

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer shall implement all the projects in the AIP to ensure utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the LDRRM Council, and the Bids and Awards Committee, shall prioritize the implementation of the LDRRM Projects within the timetable set to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the community from the occurrence of natural calamities and mitigate the risk of damage in case of disaster.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor with the LDRRM Officer shall obtain and pay for the accident insurance of all Accredited Community Disaster Volunteers (ACDVs) under the LDRRMO to protect them in case of death or injury while engaged in volunteerism activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru its Integrated Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (IMSWMB) and SWM Focal person, shall implement the activities embodied in their Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan to ensure the sustainability and effectiveness in the protection of public health and the environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the authority from the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan, shall execute Contract of Lease with duly qualified stallholders, duly notarized and with complete terms and conditions.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall strictly monitor every month, the 
collection of rental fees, including imposing the appropriate action for lessees occupying the corridor and those who sub-lease their space.

We recommended that: 

The Municipal Mayor

Require the said Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the LGU.   

The Municipal Engineer: 

Assess immediately the local roads implemented in prior years and submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant for proper adjustment on the value of road lots in the books;

Conduct an assessment every end of the year or whenever there is any indication that a component of the local road may be impaired; 

Submit a report to the Municipal Accountant and the GSO of any impairment, removal and repairs on any of the road components of the LGU.

The Municipal General Services Officer:

Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components and update the same based on the report of the Municipal Engineer on any repairs or removal done. 

Complete the reconciliation of his records with that of the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee. 


The Municipal Accountant:

Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network of the LGU; 

Reconcile the book balance with the Local Road Inventory Report, and; 


Compute the depreciated replacement cost of each local road projects implemented by the LGU based on the Appraisal Report of the Municipal Engineer.
observations.managementAction:
An assessment was made by the Municipal Engineer, and the report was still to be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant

Reports were still to be prepared and submitted to Municipal Accountant and GSO




Still waiting for the final report





Still waiting for the final report to be reconciled with the Municipal Accountant





Still waiting for the report from the Municipal Engineer and GSO


Still waiting for the report from the Municipal Engineer and GSO

Still waiting for the report from the Municipal Engineer and GSO

We also recommended that the Officer-in-Charge of Tabango Municipal Water Works System implement the governing policies in the operation of the water system and enforce collections of overdue unpaid water bills with the appropriate surcharges.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records monthly so that an accurate and reliable balance of the Cash Local Treasury account will be fairly presented in the financial statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer shall implement all the projects in the AIP to ensure utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the LDRRM Council, and the Bids and Awards Committee, shall prioritize the implementation of the LDRRM Projects within the timetable set to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the community from the occurrence of natural calamities and mitigate the risk of damage in case of disaster.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor with the LDRRM Officer shall obtain and pay for the accident insurance of all Accredited Community Disaster Volunteers (ACDVs) under the LDRRMO to protect them in case of death or injury while engaged in volunteerism activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru its Integrated Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (IMSWMB) and SWM Focal person, shall implement the activities embodied in their Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan to ensure the sustainability and effectiveness in the protection of public health and the environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the authority from the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan, shall execute Contract of Lease with duly qualified stallholders, duly notarized and with complete terms and conditions.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall strictly monitor every month, the collection of rental fees, including imposing the appropriate action for lessees occupying the corridor and those who sub-lease their space.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) reconcile the balances of each project with that of the accounting records from the provided Report of Inventory of Public Infrastructures; (2) identify the specific public infrastructures acquired prior to CY 2017, record the same to the appropriate Infrastructure Asset accounts; (3) maintain property ledger cards for each public infrastructure, and; (4) prepare a lapsing schedule to compute depreciation of each asset.
observations.managementAction:
Lack of Office Staff that would conduct the complete description and cost segregation of the public infrastructure.











Still waiting for the details from the Engineering Office, but the Municipal Accountant started to record the Public Infrastructures per available records because some of the records were destroyed from Typhoon Yolanda in CY 2013

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that: 

The Municipal Mayor 

Create an Inventory Committee who will conduct an actual inspection and listing of all road lots owned by LGU Tabango; and 

Require the said Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the LGU.                

The Municipal Engineer: 

Assess immediately the local roads implemented in prior years and submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant for proper adjustment on the value of road lots in the books;

Conduct an assessment every end of the year or whenever there is any indication that a component of the local road may be impaired; 

Submit a report to the Municipal Accountant and the GSO of any impairment, removal and repairs on any of the road components of the LGU. 

The Municipal General Services Officer: 

Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components and update the same based on the report of the Municipal Engineer on any repairs or removal done. 

Complete the reconciliation of his records with that of the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee. 

The Municipal Accountant: 

Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network of the LGU; 

Reconcile the book balance with the Local Road Inventory Report, and; 

Compute the depreciated replacement cost of each local road projects implemented by the LGU based on the Appraisal Report of the Municipal Engineer.

The Inventory Committee started to conduct the physical inventory and still on-going

Computation of depreciated replacement was not yet undertaken, pending for the Appraisal Report from the Municipal Engineer

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts to identify items which causes discrepancies between the books and the bank; and (2) prepare immediately the adjusting entries whenever errors in recording are disclosed in the monthly prepared BRS.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation was already prepared by the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries to record the assets and restore the liability due to the funding agency. Also, make sure that subsequent similar transactions are recorded in the proper accounts for fair presentation of the related accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: An adjusting entry was recorded by the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor as the Presiding Officer and the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan enact, through an Appropriation Ordinance, the Annual Budget of the Municipality within the prescribed period so as not to hamper the implementation of projects of the Municipality and provide social, economic and environmental growth for its constituents.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, avoid using generic terms in the Annual Investment Plan but instead identify specific projects so that the projects can immediately be implemented without further approval from the SB.
observations.managementAction:
The Appropriation Ordinance for Annual Budget was enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan within the prescribed period






Projects were specifically identified in the Annual Investment Plan

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that: 

 The Sangguniang Bayan authorize the Municipal Treasurer to open a new bank account for the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF to prevent its utilization other than its intended purpose.
 
The Municipal DRRMO include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, in a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years in the Trust Fund. 

The MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

The Municipal Accountant reclassify the prior year’s LDRRMF recorded as Due to LGU to its appropriate account, Trust Liability - LDRRMF.

Utilization of LDRRMF were already submitted to COA Office

We also recommended that the Officer-in-Charge of Tabango Municipal Water Works System implement the governing policies in the operation of the water system and enforce collections of overdue unpaid water bills with the appropriate surcharges.

Establish Materials Recovery Facility in the five barangays in order to facilitate the segregation of solid waste at the barangay level;  

Hire additional manpower who will be assigned primarily in the collection of garbage and make sure that garbage of all barangays accessible by the garbage truck will be regularly collected; and 

Require all barangays located in the mountainous areas to have a proper solid waste management and closely monitor its implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point and its members: (1) prepare and submit to the DILG and COA the GAD Accomplishment Report within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year to avoid sanctions for non-compliance with laws, rules and regulations; (2) submit to the Audit Team the GAD Plan and Budget within five working days from receipt of the approved plan from the DILG.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Council for the Protection of Children in the LGU to actively perform its mandated functions and formulate plans, programs and policies for the welfare of the children.   

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the created LCPC to prepare the following documents for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget: a.) LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for integration in the local development plan; b.) Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and; c.) Local Investment Plan for Children (LCC).

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant with a complete description and cost segregation for each public infrastructure project as basis in reconciling with accounting records and to ensure that all Pubic Infrastructures implemented by the LGU prior to CY 2017 are recorded in the books of accounts.
1.10 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) reconcile the balances of each project with that of the accounting records from the provided Report of Inventory of Public Infrastructures; (2) identify the specific public infrastructures acquired prior to CY 2017, record the same to the appropriate Infrastructure Asset accounts; (3) maintain property ledger cards for each public infrastructure, and; (4) prepare a lapsing schedule to compute depreciation of each asset.

2.7 We recommended that:
A. The Municipal Mayor:
1. Create an Inventory Committee who will conduct an actual
inspection and listing of all road lots owned by LGU Tabango; and
2. Require the said Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of the Road Network System of the LGU.
B. The Municipal Engineer:
1. Assess immediately the local roads implemented in prior years and
submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant for proper
adjustment on the value of road lots in the books;
2. Conduct an assessment every end of the year or whenever there is any
indication that a component of the local road may be impaired;
3. Submit a report to the Municipal Accountant and the GSO of any
impairment, removal and repairs on any of the road components of
the LGU.
C. The Municipal General Services Officer:
1. Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads
and its components and update the same based on the report of the
Municipal Engineer on any repairs or removal done.
2. Complete the reconciliation of his records with that of the Municipal
Accountant and the Inventory Committee.
D. The Municipal Accountant:
1. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network of the LGU;
2. Reconcile the book balance with the Local Road Inventory Report,
and;
3. Compute the depreciated replacement cost of each local road projects
implemented by the LGU based on the Appraisal Report of the
Municipal Engineer.

3.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts to identify items which causes discrepancies between the books and the bank; and (2) prepare immediately the adjusting entries whenever errors in recording are disclosed in the monthly prepared BRS.

4.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries to record the assets and restore the liability due to the funding agency. Also, make sure that subsequent similar transactions are recorded in the proper accounts for fair presentation of the related accounts in the financial statements.

5.11 We recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor as the Presiding Officer and the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan enact, through an Appropriation Ordinance, the Annual Budget of the Municipality within the prescribed period so as not to hamper the implementation of projects of the Municipality and provide social, economic and environmental growth for its constituents.
5.12 Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, avoid using generic terms in the Annual Investment Plan but instead identify specific projects so that the projects can immediately be implemented without further approval from the SB.

6.8 We recommended that:
5. The Sangguniang Bayan authorize the Municipal Treasurer to open a
new bank account for the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF to
prevent its utilization other than its intended purpose.
6. The Municipal DRRMO include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, in
a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded from
the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years in the Trust Fund.
7. The MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant submit the Report on
Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and Local
Development Council to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the
15th day after the end of each month.
8. The Municipal Accountant reclassify the prior years LDRRMF
recorded as Due to LGU to its appropriate account, Trust Liability -
LDRRMF.

7.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Agriculturist immediately amend the executed Memorandum of Agreement by and between the LGU of Tabango and the respective Association to include the provisions defining the accountabilities and responsibilities of both parties as regards to the distributed equipment, livestock, other agricultural supplies and interventions to ensure that the objectives of the program will be achieved for the benefit of the associations and its members.

8.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Municipal Agriculturist and the Bids and Awards Committee, fast track the procurement process of all items for the implementation of programs funded by National Government Agencies at the soonest time possible from receipt of the funds so that the objectives of the programs will be fully achieved on the desired time.
9.6 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the entire amount collected strictly within the prescribe period to protect government funds and avoid the risks of possible loss or misuse.

10.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer:
(1) collect the rentals from all delinquent stall tenants, including the
appropriate penalties, otherwise, close the establishments and impose the
appropriate sanctions; and, (2) close the stalls without business permits.
10.14 We also recommended that the Officer-in-Charge of Tabango Municipal
Water Works System implement the governing policies in the operation of the water system and enforce collections of overdue unpaid water bills with the appropriate surcharges.

11.8 We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, through the SWM Focal Person, undertake the following: (1) Establish Materials Recovery Facility in the five barangays in order to facilitate the segregation of solid waste at the barangay level; (2) Hire additional manpower who will be assigned primarily in the collection of garbage and make sure that garbage of all barangays accessible by the garbage truck will be regularly collected; and (3) Require all barangays located in the mountainous areas to have a proper solid waste management and closely monitor its implementation.

12.6 We recommended that the GAD Focal Point and its members: (1) prepare and submit to the DILG and COA the GAD Accomplishment Report within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year to avoid sanctions for non-compliance with laws, rules and regulations; (2) submit to the Audit Team the GAD Plan and Budget within five working days from receipt of the approved plan from the DILG.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the above-cited governing rules and regulations in the recording and conduct of physical inventory of property, plant and equipment and shall specifically undertake the following:
1.a) Create an inventory committee who will conduct a thorough physical count of PPE including their classification to the appropriate accounts and the assignments of the correct unit value to afford reconciliation of both records maintained in the Accounting Section and the Supply Office, the segregation of those that are no longer serviceable by class or type.
2. b) Cause the immediate submission of inventory report on physical count of PPE to the concerned Audit Team for audit, verification and validation; 
3.c) Require the Accounting and Supply Office to maintain the respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards and regularly reconcile their records pertaining to PPE balances to prove, that the assigned values to the assets per the agency’s books are reliable and accurate and to afford fair presentation of the related PPE accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management send demand letters to all accountable officers requiring immediate liquidation or settlement of outstanding cash advances which should cause the withholding of salaries and other claims due them equivalent to the unsettled amount in case such demand is unheeded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the appropriate
adjusting journal entries to record the assets, restore the liability due to the
funding agency and correct the equity account for the overstatement of the expense
accounts. Also, make sure that subsequent similar transactions are recorded in the
proper accounts for fair presentation of the related accounts in the financial
statements.

The Municipal Accountant signified that appropriate adjusting journal entry will
be prepared and effected immediately in CY 2018. Similar transactions occurring in the
future will be treated properly to afford fair presentation of the related accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require both the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records monthly so that
an accurate and reliable balance of the Cash Local Treasury account will be fairly
presented in the financial statements.

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer commented that the
recommendation will be complied. Monthly reconciliation will be conducted in their
records to arrive at an accurate and reliable balance of the Cash Local Treasury account.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor through the Local Development
Council, include as priority projects in the AIP only those which are capital in
nature as enumerated in Item 3 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2017-1.

In addition, we recommend that projects included in the AIP to be
implemented during the year must always be specifically identified for the audit
team to monitor its implementation and for the information of its constituents.

We also recommend that the Municipal Engineer ensure that all identified
priority projects in the AIP are implemented during the year to maximize the
utilization of the 20% Development Fund so as to achieve desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor cause the immediate transfer or
turnover of the 17 units agricultural equipment to its respective beneficiaries:
Brgy. Gimarco Farmers Association (BAGFA) and Villa-ramo Furtuna Corn
Farmers Organization (VILFURCOFARO) to ensure that the objective of the
YRRP to support the farmers of affected LGUs in their pursuit of agricultural
development and sustainable livelihood is carried out properly.

The Municipal Agriculturist commented that the goods should have been taken
cared of well if the municipality has a warehouse or bodega and even a covered court to
protect those goods from exposure to rain and heat of the sun. To minimize further
damaged or deterioration of those equipment, with the confirmation of the LCE, the
goods will soon be distributed to the respective Farmers Association and encourage
them for the immediate completion of their sheds to serve as shelter of these equipment.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
enforce the collection of unpaid rentals and not to allow tenants to occupy
market stalls without a Contract of Lease. Likewise, to enforce the collection of
unpaid business taxes and not to allow business establishments to operate without
securing first Mayor’s Permit and paid the corresponding business taxes and
licenses.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor impose penalties in
accordance with the pertinent provisions of the Local Ordinance No. 2014-03 for
failure to pay within the prescribed period.

Management commented that they will send demand letters to delinquent tenants
and business establishments to enforce collection of unpaid stall rentals and business
taxes.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point
System of the LGU to actively perform its mandated tasks and religiously submit
the required GAD Accomplishment Report based on reviewed GAD Plan and
Budget to ensure that implemented GAD PPAs are relevant to the identified gender
issues.

The audit recommendation was noted for compliance and management signified
to act on it.

We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board,
through the SWM Focal Person, specifically undertake the following:
a) Initiate the compliance of the recommended modifications on the SWMP
which is to update the Municipal Ordinance on SWM pursuant to R.A
9003 and other related laws and regulations;
b) Establish Materials Recovery Facility in 7 barangays to ensure approval
of the plan by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and in
order to facilitate the segregation of solid waste at the barangay level;
c) Hire additional manpower who will be assigned primarily in the
collection of garbage and make sure that all barangays accessible by the
garbage truck will be regularly collected;
d) Coordinate and require all barangays located in the mountainous areas to
have a proper solid waste management and closely monitor their
implementation.

Management will positively address the audit recommendations and necessary
assistance will be given to the different barangays in the implementation of their Solid
Waste Management.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Council for the
Protection of Children in the LGU to actively perform it’s mandated functions and
formulate plans, programs and policies for the welfare of the children.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the created LCPC to
prepare the following documents for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and
Annual Budget: a.) LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according
to needs for integration in the local development plan; b.) Local Development Plan
for Children (LDPC), and; c.) Local Investment Plan for Children (LCC).

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that 1% of
the IRA of the Municipality is appropriated for the implementation of LCPC-
identified programs, projects and activities for child protection and welfare and
prevention of juvenile delinquency.

The Municipal Budget Officer commented that the LCPC has long been created
in the LGU. Since no orientation was conducted, the council was not able to act on its
functions accordingly such as the formulation of plans, programs and policies for the
welfare of the children. However, management signified to comply with the audit
recommendations.

We recommend that the Local School Board Council incorporate in its
budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development.

The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and the Municipal
Budget Officer (MBO) will conduct further research on how to incorporate the ECCD
program in the issuing year’s budget. The MBO suggested that the LGU will construct
a facility primarily for ECCD program and the possibility of seeking financial assistance
from the Provincial Government of Leyte.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor designate a focal person to obtain copies of the MOA and other necessary documents from the funding agencies to guide the LGU in its project implementation. Require the concerned department heads to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded by the various NGAs in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the Liquidation Reports and other documents to the source agencies to facilitate succeeding release of funds for the projects.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Heads of Offices to submit an explanation as to why the priority projects were not implemented. Also, to ensure that all identified priority projects in the AIP are implemented during the year to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal DRRMO include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, in a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years in the Trust Fund. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRMO, implement the projects in the LDRRMFIP which are intended to contribute in strengthening the capacity of the LGU to respond to disasters.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal School Board, through its Chairman, undertake the following measures: a) Include as topmost priority the improvement of educational facilities to maximize the purpose of the Special Education Fund and ensure that optimum benefits for the schools is achieved. b) Stop the grant of cash advances to officers and employees who are not bonded. c) Require the immediate submission of Liquidation Reports when the purpose for which the cash advance was granted has been served. d) Utilize SEF exclusively for purposes for which the fund was created.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board initiate the immediate construction of the Materials Recovery Facility and sustain the effective implementation of the LGU’s Solid Waste Management Plan. Also, ascertain that all barangay have MRFs and solid waste bins, with regular collection schedule of their solid waste. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the full implementation of the SWM projects integrated in the 20% Development Fund and ensure effective and efficient enforcement of environmental ordinances towards the attainment of the objectives of the LGU’s Ten-Year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction: AAR Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record procured medicines and supplies in the appropriate inventory account upon purchase to recognize in the books the asset and record the related expense upon its issuance for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office maintain Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards for each type of inventory item, to monitor receipts and issuance and reconcile the respective balances monthly.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management send demand letters to all accountable officers requiring immediate liquidation or settlement of outstanding cash advances which should cause the withholding of salaries and other claims due them in case such demand is unheeded.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented.
observations.reason: AAR Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management undertake the following: a) Create an inventory committee who will conduct a thorough physical count of PPE including their classification to the appropriate accounts; b) Cause the immediate submission of inventory report on physical count of PPE to the concerned Audit Team for audit, verification and validation; c) Require the Accounting and Supply Office to maintain the respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards and regularly reconcile their records pertaining to PPE balances to prove, that the assigned values to the assets per the agency’s books are reliable and accurate and to afford fair presentation of the related PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented.
observations.reason: Conduct of physical count of PPE still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for management to strictly adhere to the above-cited governing rules and regulations and specifically undertake the following: 1. Cause the immediate creation/establishment of the GAD Focal Point System to accelerate gender mainstreaming and formulation/enhance ment and implementation of the Local GAD Code or Ordinances. 2. Prepare annual GAD Plan and Budget and have it reviewed and duly approved by the DILG Regional Office and incorporate in the Annual Budget with equivalent cost of 5% of its total amount. 3. Prepare and submit to the Audit Team GAD accomplishment report based on the reviewed and duly approved GAD Plan and Budget to ensure implementation of GAD PPAs which are relevant to the identified gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of the above-cited rules and regulations and specifically undertake the following: a.) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to be serious in complying the audit recommendations on complete submission of accounts within the prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of each month since this has been a finding in the CY 2013 & 2014 AR. b) Submit other needed reports required by the audit team as representative of the Commission on Audit, to facilitate on time audit and verification on the propriety and validity of the consummated transactions as well as the accuracy and reliability of the accounts recorded and presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the provisions of Sec. 70 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol I, and Sec. 347 of R.A 7160.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct an inventory of all parcels of land owned by the municipality and to maintain Real Property Card for each piece of land. Likewise, these properties should be registered in the name of the municipality with the Registry of Deeds and duly supported with the proof of absolute ownership such as the certificate of land title.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record procured medicines
and supplies in the appropriate inventory account upon purchase to recognize in the
books the asset and record the related expense upon its issuance for fair
presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services
Office maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards respectively, for each type
of inventory item, to monitor receipts and issuance and reconcile the respective
balances monthly.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor designate a focal person to obtain
copies of the MOA and other necessary documents from the funding agencies to
guide the LGU in its project implementation. Require the concerned department
heads to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded by the
various NGAs in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA.

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the
Liquidation Reports and other documents to the source agencies to facilitate
succeeding release of funds for the projects.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Heads of
Offices to fast track the implementation of the projects. Also, to ensure that all
identified priority projects in the AIP are implemented during the year to maximize
the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and achieve desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes.

We recommend that the Municipal DRRMO include in the LDRRMF
Investment Plan, in a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded
from the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years in the Trust Fund.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRMO,
implement the projects in the LDRRMFIP which are intended to contribute in
strengthening the capacity of the LGU to respond to disasters.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board, through its Chairman,
adhere strictly with the pertinent provisions of RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG
Joint Circular in the utilization of the SEF funds and undertake the following:

a) Include as topmost priority the improvement of educational facilities to
maximize the purpose of the Special Education Fund and ensure that
optimum benefits for the schools is achieved.

b) Stop the grant of cash advances to officers and employees who are not
bonded.

c) Require the immediate submission of Liquidation Reports from the
concerned accountable officers when the purpose for which the cash
advance was granted has been served.

d) Utilize SEF exclusively for purposes for which the fund was created.

We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board initiate
the immediate construction of the central Materials Recovery Facility and
procurement of Garbage Truck and sustain the effective implementation of the
LGU’s Solid Waste Management Plan. Also, ascertain that all barangays have
MRFs and solid waste bins, with regular collection schedule of their solid waste.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the full
implementation of the SWM projects integrated in the 20% Development Fund and
ensure effective and efficient enforcement of environmental ordinances towards the
attainment of the objectives of the LGU’s Ten-Year Ecological Solid Waste
Management Plan.

and shall specifically
instruct the designated
BAC Chairman and the
BAC Secretariat to always
submit perfected
contracts together
with the required
supporting documents
within the prescribed
period of five (5) days
from date of perfection.
observations.reason: Designated BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat were not always submitting perfected contracts together with the required supporting documents within the prescribed period of five (5) days.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
management strictly
adhere to the
pertinent provisions
of the revised IRR of
R.A. 9184
particularly in the
resort to alternative
mode of procurement,
in this case, the
Shopping mode and
shall undertake the
following corrective
measures:
a) Procurement thru
the alternative mode,
in this case thru the
Shopping, must be
supported with a BAC
Resolution indicating
the reason why they
resort to the said
alternative mode duly
approved by the Head
of the Procuring Entity.
b) Always support
procurement thru
Shopping of commonly
use supplies and
equipment with PS
Certificate of non-availability
of stocks to prevent
disallowed transactions.
c) Request for
amounts more than the
prescribed threshold
of P50,000.00 for
Shopping procurement
shall be done thru
Competitive Bidding.
observations.managementAction: Procurements implement ed thru Shopping; amounts still not in accordance with the Annual Procurement Plan. PS Certificates not always obtained.
observations.reason: Some procurements were done thru Shopping even though amounts exceeded thresholds; lack of PS Certificates and not always following Annual Procurement Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
management strictly
adhere to the
pertinent provisions
on complete
documentation as
provided on the
above – cited laws,
rules and regulations
on the payment of
allowances and shall
instruct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure
that supporting
documents were
complete before
processing the
following claims:
a) payment of monthly
RATA must be
supported with DTRs
for appointive officials
for easy determination
of absences, likewise
for the elective
officials, adopted work
schedule and copy of
the minutes of the
sessions conducted and
such other documents
necessary to support
the claim.
b) Payment of
subsistence and
laundry allowance to
public health workers
must be supported
with DTRs.
c) Payment of Hazard
Pay for public health
workers must be
supported with DTRs
and certification or
approval from the
Secretary of Health.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to ensure complete supporting documents prior to processing claims.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend for
management to strictly
adhere to the
governing Local
Disaster Risk
Reduction Management
Fund (LDRRMF) and
specifically undertake
the following:
a) Instruct the
Municipal Accountant
on regularly submit the
Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF
using the format in
Annex B of COA
Circular 2012-002 to
the regular auditor of
the LGU.
b) In the interest of
transparency in all
transactions, instruct
the concerned
personnel of the LGU
to always support with
copy of all releases and
uses of Local Disaster
Risk Reduction
Management Fund for
programs, projects and
activities listed in the
approved plan and all
the necessary
documentation on the
disbursements amounting
to P2,759,847.75 to
afford the validity and
propriety of the
transactions.
c) Cause the
immediate transfer of
the unexpended
balance of the QRF
and DRRM-MOOE
from CY 2011 to 2015
in the total amount of
P4,340,338.28 to the
Special Trust Fund in
the Trust Fund books
to ensure proper
utilization of the fund
for its intended
purpose.
observations.managementAction: LDRRM Officer commented that the P1.2 million unimplemented appropriations under the 70% Mitigation Fund include the P900,000.00 Flood Control Projects which are currently in the completion stages after the issuance of Notice to Proceed on December 26, 2016. The Council eventually approved the Supplemental LDRRMIP and made a resolution (MDRRMC Resolution 2017-01) endorsing to the Sangguniang Bayan the approval of the supplemental LDRRMF CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
management to adhere
to the pertinent
provisions contained
in the above- stated
Circular particularly
on the optimum
utilization of the
fund to help achieve
desirable socio-
economic development
and environmental
outcomes and shall
specifically:
1. Cause the
immediate full
implementation of the
identified priority
projects contained in
the CY 2015 AIP which
partakes the nature
“Reiterated in Audit
Observation No. 4 of
the AAR.”
2. Instruct the
Municipal Accountant
to maintain a
Subsidiary Ledgers for
the 20% development
fund to account all
disbursements and
balances of the fund.
observations.managementAction: MPDO, they implement still depend on the Mayor’s approval for projects. The Municipal Accountant shall maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the 20% development fund.
observations.reason: Management was busy in preparation of Solid Waste Management Plan and did not properly implement the projects for utilization of the 20% Development Fund; concerned Department Heads will look into reasons for non-implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
management to strictly
adhere to the
guidelines for the
preparation of annual
Gender and
Development (GAD)
Plans, Budgets and
Accomplishment
Reports, among others,
to implement the
guidelines on the
localization of the
Magna Carta of Women
as provided in Joint
Circular No. 2013-01
issued by the DILG,
DBM and NEDA and
shall specifically
undertake the
following:
1. Cause the
immediate creation/
establishment of the
GAD Focal Point
System to accelerate
gender mainstreaming
and formulation/
enhancement and
implementation of the
Local GAD Code or
Ordinances.
2. Prepare annual
GAD Plan and Budget
and have it reviewed
and duly approved by
the DILG Regional
Office and submit to
the Audit Team GAD
accomplishment
report based on the
reviewed and duly
approved GAD Plan
and Budget to ensure
implementation of GAD
PPAs which are
relevant to the
identified gender
issues.
observations.managementAction: Management did not incorporate in the reviewed GAD Annual Budget with equivalent cost of 5% of its total amount. The Management prepares GAD plan and budget and submits to DILG. The DILG has not yet reviewed/approved.
observations.reason: Management did not submit reviewed and approved GAD Plan and Budget to DILG; Local Chief Executive had not signed appropriate issuances to institutionalize the GAD Focal Point System.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
management should
adhere with the payment
of checks directly to
payees except for small
value items that could
be paid out of petty
cash fund. Stop the
practice of
reimbursement and
strict adherence to the
procurement law thus,
reasonableness of the
prices being paid for
and existence of the
procured goods and
services. Moreover, all
disbursements must be
supported with complete
documentation.
observations.reason: Stop the practice of reimbursement and strict adherence to procurement law is hereby enjoined.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
management strictly
adhere to the
provisions of the
above-cited rules and
regulations and
specifically undertake
the following:
a.) Instruct the
Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer
to be serious in
complying the audit
recommendations on
the on-time submission
of accounts within the
prescribed period of
ten (10) days after the
end of each month.
b) Submit other
needed reports required
by the audit team as
to facilitate on time
audit and verification
on the propriety and
validity of the
consummated
transactions as well as
the accuracy and
reliability of the
accounts recorded and
presented in the
financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer does not comply with the on-time submission of accounts to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer thereby delaying the submission of accounts to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Delay in submission of accounts and supporting schedules; Municipal Treasurer and Accountant not timely in providing documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
management strictly
adhere to the pertinent
provisions of COA
rules in the recording
of purchased supplies
and materials and
drugs, and medicines
and the maintenance
of the prescribed
Supplies Ledger Cards,
Stock Cards per
inventory item with
reconciled balances to
afford fairness in their
presentation in the
financial statements as
at given period.
(b) Instruct the
Municipal GSO to
prepare Purchase
Request for purchases
of supplies and materials
based on the needed
supplies and materials
for the quarter of the
respective department
per approved Annual
Procurement Plan.
(c) Instruct the
Municipal GSO and
Municipal Accountant
to have all issuances
of supplies and
materials, drugs and
medicines covered by
Requisition Issue Slip
(RIS) which will
support the Report of
Supplies and Materials
Issued (RSMI) to be
prepared weekly and
submitted to the
Accounting Office as
basis for recording.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented. Procured supplies and materials were still directly debited to the related expense account. Issuances not covered with RIS and RSMI not prepared.
observations.reason: Not Implemented; supplies still not recorded per required inventory records; RSMI not prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
management strictly
adhere to the pertinent
provisions of the
Implementing Rules
and Regulations of
R.A. 9003, a Municipal
Solid Waste
Management Board
shall be created who
will develop a
Municipal Solid Waste
Management Plan that
shall ensure the long-
term management of
solid waste, as well as
integrate the various
solid waste management
plans and strategies of
the barangays in its
area of jurisdiction for
the assurance of
protection of public
health and environment
and to perform other
mandated functions to
relative to Solid Waste
Management.
observations.managementAction: Management developed the Solid Waste Management Plan but failed to implement segregation of waste and full schedule of garbage collection.
observations.reason: They developed the plan but failed to implement the municipal MRFs and the full garbage collection schedule.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the
Municipal Mayor should
require the Municipal
Accountant to strictly
adhere to the provisions
of Sec. 70 of NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Vol I
and Sec. 347 of RA
7160 and ensure timely
preparation and
submission of trial
balances and financial
statements.
observations.reason: Monthly and quarterly trial balances were not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
management should
create a committee that
will conduct the
physical inventory of
buildings and other
structural properties of
the agency in order to
insure said properties
with the GSIS and
pursuant to P.D 612 and
Section 489 for the
purpose of protecting
these assets from the
risks and destruction
that may be brought by
any fortuitous events
in the form of
indemnification for the
benefit and the best
interest of the
government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still in the process of identifying the buildings and structural properties to be insured with the GSIS.
observations.reason: Still in the process identifying the parcels to be insured; implementation will take substantial time and resources.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
management conduct an
inventory of all parcels
of land owned by the
municipality and to
maintain Real Property
Card for each piece of
land, instruct the
municipality to proceed
with the titling of these
parcels through the
LAMP survey and
titling program and
comply with the
requirements to support
proof of absolute
ownership such as the
certificate of land
title.
observations.managementAction: Considering that the titling of these parcels of land is still in the process under the LAMP program, the municipality will take the necessary actions to follow-up the substantial survey and titling through the LAMP program; the municipality had instructed the assessor to survey and comply with needed documents.
observations.reason: Titling of parcels is still in process of compliance with required documents; substantial time needed.
